key_insight: A hybrid IFENN approach can learn phase-field fracture initiation and propagation on a benchmark geometry and generalize to unseen geometries by coupling FEM with two pretrained neural operators specialized for different fracture stages.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, lower-cost surrogate modeling of fracture growth in computational mechanics while preserving physics-based coupling for more realistic design and damage prediction.
method: FEM solves mechanical equilibrium in the coupled system
DeepOKAN models phase-field diffusion during crack initiation
CNN models phase-field diffusion during crack propagation
evidence_signal: The networks are trained once on a benchmark case using a physics-informed strategy with very few increments and limited Gauss-point samples, then evaluated on both training and unseen geometries.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of far-field correction via artificial boundary conditions, especially when extrapolating DeepOKAN predictions away from the crack tip.
paper_title: A hybrid IFENN solver for generalizable modeling of phase-field fracture initiation and propagation
